acceptance_targets: [chapter_07_booker_001] Reading the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:88, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:234, each June-contact beat must produce a distinct consequence (new information, changed decision, or immediate cost); fail if any two adjacent beats repeat only voicemail-plus-remorse without a new external stake.

[chapter_07_craft_001] In the revised voicemail anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:188 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:192, pass only if Eli names at least two concrete harms with observable consequences and avoids abstract catch-all remorse phrasing (for example "broke enough", "messed everything up").

[chapter_07_craft_002] In the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:254 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:255, fail if the prose undercuts its own image with disclaimers (for example "melodramatic" or equivalent self-correction); pass if the emotional force is carried forward through concrete detail.

[FBK-02-001] PASS if, in the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2172 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2266, a reader can identify Nora vs June without dialogue tags in at least two consecutive exchanges; FAIL if both voices still rely on the same amount/proof command syntax.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:88, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:234, each June-contact beat must produce a distinct consequence (new information, changed decision, or immediate cost); fail if any two adjacent beats repeat only voicemail-plus-remorse without a new external stake.
findings.problem: The chapter runs four similar June-contact beats (call, voicemail, self-correction, shame) with limited new leverage until late, so the middle movement loops instead of escalating scene consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or merge one of the voicemail cycles around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:88, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:96, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:194, and replace it with a concrete new road cost (money, time, safety, or missed lead) before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:234.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised voicemail anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:188 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:192, pass only if Eli names at least two concrete harms with observable consequences and avoids abstract catch-all remorse phrasing (for example "broke enough", "messed everything up").
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: in a high-accountability moment, Eli defaults to abstract remorse ("I know I broke enough") instead of naming the concrete harms he caused, which functions as a moral safety valve.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the voicemail at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:188, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:192, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:193 so Eli names specific actions and consequences (for example money transfer harm, deadline harm, and boundary harm) instead of generic damage language.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:254 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:255, fail if the prose undercuts its own image with disclaimers (for example "melodramatic" or equivalent self-correction); pass if the emotional force is carried forward through concrete detail.
findings.problem: Timidity: the narration introduces a hard image (handcuffs) and immediately retreats by labeling it melodramatic, reducing pressure right when the route decision should feel most coercive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:254 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:255 to commit to either a single charged image or a fully concrete physical/economic rendering of compulsion, without self-canceling commentary.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2172 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2266, a reader can identify Nora vs June without dialogue tags in at least two consecutive exchanges; FAIL if both voices still rely on the same amount/proof command syntax.
findings.problem: High-pressure dialogue defaults to shared procedural diction ("dates and amounts", "say the number", "something is not an amount"), flattening idiolect separation between Nora, Eli, and June and creating a scripted register drift.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the exchanges at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2172, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2192, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2266, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2292 so Nora keeps ICU-triage precision while June keeps younger, socially compressed campus phrasing; remove repeated command-template phrasing and add interruptions/false starts unique to each speaker.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
